revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:浄楽寺・七ツ塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:浄楽寺・七ツ塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:浄楽寺・七ツ塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[ja:浄楽寺・七ツ塚古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster | native_name = 浄楽寺・七ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Jyoraku-ji and Nanatsuduka Kofun group.JPG|image= File:Jyoraku-ji and Nanatsuduka Kofun group.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|33|13|N|133|18|44|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|34.763611111111|N|132.89888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|en|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|qid=Q840591|lt=Miyoshi, Hiroshima|1=Miyoshi, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Miyoshi|qid=Q840591|1=Miyoshi, Hiroshima}}| region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th-6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|13|N|133|18|44|E}}}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster'''|浄楽寺・七ツ塚古墳群| Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Takasugi and Odasaiwai neighborhoods of the city of {{ill|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|en|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|qid=Q840591|lt=Miyoshi|1=Miyoshi, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The cluster was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1972.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139274|title=浄楽寺・七ツ塚古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 May 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyoshi Basin in the [[:en:Chugoku Mountains|Chugoku Mountains]] of former {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|qid=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}} has one of the most dense concentration of Kofuns in the [[:en:Chugoku region|Chugoku region]]. The Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka cluster is one of the largest of these, and is located on a hill on the west bank of the Mihara River in the southeastern part of the Miyoshi Basin. The Kofuns are divided into north and south groups. In the Jōrakuji cluster on the north side consists of a total of 116 ''kofun'', including one {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hotategata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}}), 97 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and 18 {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}). Mound No.12. the largest in this cluster, is a circular Kofun measuring 6 meters in height and 55 meters in diameter.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the Nanatsuzuka Kofuns, located approximately 116 meters south, consists of a total of 60 Kofuns, including one {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 29.5 meters. There are also 2 {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped ''kofun''|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, 55 {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''empun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type ''hōfun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Based on the style of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and [[archaeological excavation|excavated artifacts]], it is believed that these Kofuns were constructed in the middleto late Kofun period (5th to 6th centuries AD). The area around the Kofuns has been preserved and maintained as the {{nihongo|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park|広島県立みよし風土記の丘||}}, which houses the Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore. The site is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kamisugi Station (Hiroshima)|en|Kamisugi Station (Hiroshima)|qid=Q11590345|lt=Kamisugi Station|1=Kamisugi Station (Hiroshima)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Geibi Line|en|Geibi Line|qid=Q841404|lt=Geibi Line|1=Geibi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|qid=Q9644839|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Hiroshima prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki No Oka Park}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120110.html Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education] {{in lang|ja}}
